wines.description: Arianna Occhipinti's debut cuvee, named for the SP68 road through Vittoria: Frappato-dominant, mineral, light-bodied, one of Italy's most celebrated natural wines.

verified.address_quoted: SP68 VITTORIA-PEDALINO KM 3,3 - 97019 VITTORIA (RG) SICILY ITALY

wines.name: Occhipinti SP68 Bianco
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel fermentation and aging; partial skin contact

taste.summary: The white counterpart to SP68 Rosso: Grecanico and Zibibbo from volcanic soils, aromatically intense, mineral and saline with a citrus-peach freshness.

pairings.why: Salinity and citrus acidity are the classic companions for raw crustaceans and molluscs.
The wine's mineral-citrus profile mirrors the lemon-dressed octopus.
Aromatic Zibibbo and herbal Grecanico complement char-grilled summer vegetables.

history.summary: Occhipinti introduced the white SP68 to mirror the red, blending native Grecanico with Zibibbo from her organically farmed Vittoria plots. It shares the road-name identity and natural-wine ethos of the red, adding aromatic depth from the Zibibbo grape.

milestones.event: First SP68 Bianco from Grecanico and Zibibbo

wines.description: Arianna Occhipinti's organic white blend of Grecanico and Zibibbo: aromatic, saline and mineral from Vittoria volcanic soils.

verified.address_quoted: SP68 VITTORIA-PEDALINO KM 3,3 - 97019 VITTORIA (RG) SICILY ITALY

wines.name: Occhipinti Siccagno
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large old oak casks, 12 months

taste.summary: Siccagno is the Sicilian dry-farming term for unirrigated cultivation; Occhipinti's varietal Nero d'Avola is richly dark-fruited with a characteristic iron-mineral finish from Vittoria's ferrous soils.

pairings.why: Full body and firm tannins stand up to rich goat meat; herby-mineral wine character mirrors the olive notes.
The wine's dark fruit and spice complement the sweet-sour Sicilian aubergine relish.
Firm tannins cut through lamb fat with warm mineral notes complementing the char.

history.summary: Siccagno takes its name from the traditional Sicilian dry-farming practice without irrigation. Occhipinti produces this wine from Nero d'Avola vines farmed without irrigation in the Vittoria zone, producing concentrated low-yield fruit that ages beautifully.

milestones.event: First vintage of Siccagno from unirrigated Nero d'Avola

wines.description: Occhipinti's dry-farmed (siccagno) Nero d'Avola: concentrated dark fruit, iron-mineral depth and firm structure from organically farmed Vittoria vines.

verified.address_quoted: SP68 VITTORIA-PEDALINO KM 3,3 - 97019 VITTORIA (RG) SICILY ITALY

wines.name: Occhipinti Il Frappato
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large old chestnut and oak casks, 8-10 months

taste.summary: Occhipinti's varietal Frappato is perhaps the finest expression of the variety: achingly delicate, chalk-mineral, fresh raspberry and rose-hip with extraordinary tension for its light weight.

pairings.why: Serve slightly cool; the wine's lightness and acidity work across clams, prawns and squid.
Frappato's delicate red fruit and mineral acidity complement raw tuna.
Low tannin and pure fruit make this a gentle pairing with fresh or soft ripened cheeses.

history.summary: Occhipinti has produced varietal Frappato since her first vintage in 2004, establishing it alongside the SP68 blend as the dual calling-card of the estate. The single-varietal bottling shows the unique qualities of the grape without the grounding of Nero d'Avola.

milestones.event: First varietal Il Frappato bottled alongside SP68

wines.description: Arianna Occhipinti's varietal Frappato: the most refined expression of this Vittoria native grape, mineral, delicate and hauntingly fresh.

verified.address_quoted: SP68 VITTORIA-PEDALINO KM 3,3 - 97019 VITTORIA (RG) SICILY ITALY

wines.name: Occhipinti Bombolieri
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large old oak casks, 12 months

taste.summary: Occhipinti's top-tier DOCG from the Bombolieri single vineyard: equal parts Frappato and Nero d'Avola in their most complex and age-worthy expression, mineral-driven with remarkable persistence.

pairings.why: The wine's mineral depth and dried-fruit notes are classical companions for roasted game birds.
Medium tannin and high acidity balance the richness of braised lamb with briny olives.
Spiced orange peel and iron in the wine bridge cured-meat spice perfectly.

history.summary: The Bombolieri is named for the vineyard parcel where Occhipinti grows her oldest Frappato and Nero d'Avola vines. It represents the premium expression of Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG from the estate, produced only in superior vintages.

milestones.event: First Bombolieri DOCG produced from the single-vineyard parcel

wines.description: Occhipinti's single-vineyard Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG: equal Frappato and Nero d'Avola of exceptional mineral complexity and age-worthiness.

verified.address_quoted: SP68 VITTORIA-PEDALINO KM 3,3 - 97019 VITTORIA (RG) SICILY ITALY

wines.name: Valle dell'Acate Cerasuolo di Vittoria Classico DOCG
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large Slavonian oak casks

taste.summary: Valle dell'Acate's classic DOCG blend from six generations of Jacono family organic farming: bright cherry and dried-herb aromatics with firm Vittoria-terroir acidity and mineral persistence.

pairings.why: Classic Southern Italian red meat ragu finds its benchmark match in Cerasuolo di Vittoria.
The wine's herbal notes mirror the herb stuffing; medium tannin supports the lean meat.
Frappato's brightness lifts the sweet-sour relish while Nero d'Avola holds the pork.

history.summary: The Jacono family has farmed 70 hectares at Acate in the Ragusa province for six generations. Gaetana Jacono began bottling their Cerasuolo di Vittoria under estate label in the late 1990s, establishing one of the DOCG's most respected organic producers.

milestones.event: Estate bottling of Cerasuolo di Vittoria under Valle dell'Acate label begins
Receives Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG classification

wines.description: Six-generation family estate's organic Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG: cherry, herbs and mineral Vittoria clay-limestone character.

wines.name: Valle dell'Acate Il Frappato
wines.oak_regime: Aged in stainless steel to preserve aromatic freshness

taste.summary: Valle dell'Acate's varietal Frappato shows the grape at its most approachable: vibrant raspberry and violet aromas, light body, zero oak, a wine for fresh enjoyment.

pairings.why: Light body and fresh acidity make this the ideal Sicilian pizza companion.
Frappato's raspberry freshness complements ripe tomatoes and fresh mozzarella.
A versatile summer red that works across light grilled vegetable dishes.

history.summary: Valle dell'Acate introduced a varietal Frappato alongside the DOCG blend to showcase the grape's individual character. Steel-only aging preserves the fresh, aromatic, lightest expression of Frappato.

milestones.event: First varietal Il Frappato from organic estate Frappato vineyards

wines.description: Valle dell'Acate's fresh and unoaked varietal Frappato: aromatic, light-bodied, raspberry and violet, an everyday Sicilian red.

wines.name: Valle dell'Acate Tane
wines.oak_regime: Aged in second-fill French barriques, 12 months

taste.summary: Valle dell'Acate's varietal Nero d'Avola shows the grape's fuller, warmer side: ripe dark fruit with vanilla-spice from French oak, round tannins and a warming Sicilian sun-drenched finish.

pairings.why: Full-bodied dark fruit and warm spice stand up to the richness of a slow braise.
Round tannins and dark cherry complement char-grilled beef.
Vanilla oak notes and dark fruit pair well with the sharp saltiness of aged Sicilian cheese.

history.summary: Tane is named in Sicilian dialect and represents Valle dell'Acate's fuller oak-aged expression of Nero d'Avola, showing the grape's capacity for richness and warmth when aged in French barriques.

milestones.event: First Tane Nero d'Avola produced with barrique aging

wines.description: Valle dell'Acate's barrel-aged Nero d'Avola: full-bodied, dark-fruited and warm-spiced from the Ragusa-province organic estate.

wines.name: Valle dell'Acate Grillo
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel only

taste.summary: Valle dell'Acate's Grillo captures the freshness of the Sicilian coast: citrus blossom, peach and herb aromas with bright acidity and clean mineral finish.

pairings.why: High acidity cuts through the oil; citrus-herb notes complement the fresh seafood.
Clean mineral profile and medium body match well with delicate seafood risotto.
Refreshing acidity and sea-breeze aromatics amplify marinated seafood.

history.summary: Valle dell'Acate added a Grillo to their range to showcase the variety's aromatic freshness from their warm Ragusa-province soils, offering an approachable organic white alongside the red lineup.

milestones.event: First Grillo from organic estate vineyards

wines.description: Valle dell'Acate's unoaked organic Grillo: fresh, citrus-herbal, bright white for summer seafood drinking.

wines.name: Gulfi Nerojbleo
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large Slavonian oak casks, 12-14 months

taste.summary: Gulfi's entry-level single-contrada Nero d'Avola from the Ibleo plateau: concentrated dark fruit, carob and licorice with firm tannin and a warm volcanic-clay mineral finish.

pairings.dish: Braised pork with black olives and capers
pairings.why: Firm tannin and dark fruit stand up to rich pork braised in the island's classic olive-caper combination.
A full-bodied red finds natural harmony with slow-cooked meat sauces.
Dark cherry and licorice notes complement char-grilled beef steak.

history.summary: Gulfi was founded around 1999 with a mission to produce authentic terroir-driven Nero d'Avola from Pachino and the Ibleo plateau. Nerojbleo was the first bottling, named for the ibleo geological plateau where the estate's pre-phylloxera Pachino vines grow.

milestones.event: First Nerojbleo bottled from Pachino-area Nero d'Avola

wines.description: Gulfi's flagship Eloro DOC Nero d'Avola from Pachino's ibleo plateau: concentrated, dark-fruited and warm with volcanic mineral depth.

verified.address_quoted: C. Maccari, Strada Prov. Noto-Pachino, KM 13.5, 96017 Noto (SR)

wines.name: Gulfi Nerobufaleffj
wines.oak_regime: Aged in new and second-fill French barriques, 18 months

taste.summary: The Bufaleffj contrada produces Gulfi's most powerful Nero d'Avola: dense, iron-mineral, dark-fruited and structured with high tannins needing several years to integrate.

pairings.why: High tannin and iron-mineral depth are classic partners for sharp aged sheep's-milk cheese.
The density and structure of this wine requires the richness of long-roasted lamb.
Dark fruit compote and espresso notes enhance the gamey richness of wild boar.

history.summary: Bufaleffj refers to a historical contrada name near Pachino. The pre-phylloxera vines in this plot produce Gulfi's most mineral and powerful Nero d'Avola, bottled separately from the estate's other single-contrada wines.

milestones.event: Single-contrada Bufaleffj Nero d'Avola first bottled

wines.description: Gulfi's Bufaleffj single-contrada Nero d'Avola: dense, high-tannin, iron-mineral and age-worthy from pre-phylloxera Pachino vines.

verified.address_quoted: C. Maccari, Strada Prov. Noto-Pachino, KM 13.5, 96017 Noto (SR)

wines.name: Gulfi Nerobaronj
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large Slavonian oak casks and French barriques, 16 months

taste.summary: The Baronj contrada produces a more herbal-mineral Nero d'Avola than Bufaleffj, with a finer-grained tannic structure and exceptional length.

pairings.why: Herbal mineral profile and firm tannin perfectly complement herb-braised rabbit.
The wine's structure and dark fruit stand up to charred, dry-aged beef.
Rich wild boar sauce needs the depth and structure of this single-contrada wine.

history.summary: Baronj is another of the historical Pachino contrade where Gulfi identified pre-phylloxera Nero d'Avola vines. Each contrada bottling explores the micro-terroir variations across the ibleo plateau.

milestones.event: Single-contrada Baronj Nero d'Avola first bottled

wines.description: Gulfi's Baronj contrada Nero d'Avola: herbal-mineral, firm-structured and age-worthy from historic Pachino ibleo-plateau parcels.

verified.address_quoted: C. Maccari, Strada Prov. Noto-Pachino, KM 13.5, 96017 Noto (SR)

wines.name: Gulfi Nerosanlore
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large Slavonian oak casks, 14 months

taste.summary: Sanlorenzo is the most elegant of Gulfi's quartet: the highest-altitude contrada delivers a more refined palate structure with exceptional aromatic complexity.

pairings.why: Elegant structure and dark fruit make this the most food-versatile of the four contrada wines.
The wine's depth and mineral character stand up to truffle-enhanced beef.
Rich fruit and elegant tannin bridge the crystalline sharpness of aged hard cheese.

history.summary: Sanlorenzo, named for the Contrada San Lorenzo parcel, was added slightly later than the initial Gulfi trio. It occupies the most elevated position in the Pachino zone and often produces the longest-lived of the single-contrada bottlings.

milestones.event: First Nerosanlore from Contrada San Lorenzo Pachino Nero d'Avola

wines.description: Gulfi's most elegant contrada Nero d'Avola from the high-altitude San Lorenzo parcel: dense fruit, refined tannin, exceptional complexity.

verified.address_quoted: C. Maccari, Strada Prov. Noto-Pachino, KM 13.5, 96017 Noto (SR)

wines.name: Feudo Montoni Vrucara
wines.oak_regime: Aged in second-fill French and Slavonian oak casks, 18 months

taste.summary: Vrucara is from Feudo Montoni's oldest pre-phylloxera Nero d'Avola vines in the high plateau of central Sicily: austere, iron-mineral, demanding years in bottle to reveal its full complexity.

pairings.why: High tannin and acidity require the richness and time of a long braise to find harmony.
Iron mineral and tobacco notes match the cured character of aged pork charcuterie.
Only the most structured red meats can stand up to Vrucara's density and tannin.

history.summary: Fabio Sireci's Feudo Montoni sits in the hills of Cammarata in the Agrigento province at 700 metres. The Vrucara comes from old pre-phylloxera Nero d'Avola vines using the traditional alberello training. It is considered one of Sicily's most demanding and distinctive terroir expressions of Nero d'Avola.

milestones.event: First commercial vintage of Vrucara from pre-phylloxera vines
Feudo Montoni receives organic certification for estate vineyards

wines.description: Feudo Montoni's single-vineyard Nero d'Avola from pre-phylloxera vines at 700 metres in central Sicily: austere, iron-mineral and among Sicily's most age-worthy reds.

wines.name: Feudo Montoni Lagnusa
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large Slavonian oak casks, 12 months

taste.summary: The more approachable Feudo Montoni Nero d'Avola; less austere than Vrucara, with a cherry-herb character and the estate's distinctive high-altitude mineral warmth.

pairings.why: Classic Sicilian pasta with fried aubergine finds its natural red wine in this warm-fruited Nero d'Avola.
Medium tannin and herbal notes complement herb-seasoned lamb.
Warm dark fruit is a classic pairing with sharp sheep's-milk aged cheese.

history.summary: Lagnusa is Feudo Montoni's entry-level Nero d'Avola, offering access to the estate's distinctive high-altitude style at an everyday price. It shares the organic farming approach and central-Sicilian terroir of the Vrucara, in a more immediately approachable profile.

milestones.event: First Lagnusa Nero d'Avola produced as approachable estate red

wines.description: Feudo Montoni's approachable Nero d'Avola from central Sicily: cherry, herb and warm mineral character from high-altitude organic vines.

wines.name: Feudo Montoni Grillo della Timpa
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel only; cold fermentation

taste.summary: Grillo grown at altitude in central Sicily develops an unusual aromatic tension; this steel-only white is crisper and more mineral than coastal Grillos with notable herbal lift.

pairings.dish: Grilled sea bass with lemon and capers
pairings.why: High acidity and citrus notes are the natural choice for simply grilled Mediterranean fish.
Clean mineral finish and high acid cut through fried squid without competing.
Herbal lift and citrus complement the brine and oil of marinated anchovies.

history.summary: Feudo Montoni expanded into white wine with the Grillo della Timpa to offer a complementary white to the estate's red range, grown on the high Cammarata plateau where cooler nights preserve aromatic freshness in the variety.

milestones.event: First Grillo della Timpa from high-altitude Cammarata organic vineyards

wines.description: Feudo Montoni's high-altitude organic Grillo: mineral, citrus-herbal and crisp, distinct from coastal examples due to its inland terroir.

wines.name: Morgante Nero d'Avola
wines.oak_regime: Aged in stainless steel and large oak casks

taste.summary: Morgante's estate Nero d'Avola from the Agrigento interior shows the full-bodied, warm-fruited character of the grape from hot-climate inland soils, with approachable tannin and immediate appeal.

pairings.why: Fresh pork sausage with fennel seeds matches the warm fruit and medium tannin of this entry red.
Full body and warm fruit are classic pizza companions.
An everyday Sicilian red that suits simple tomato-based pasta dishes.

history.summary: The Morgante family has farmed Nero d'Avola near Grotte in the Agrigento province since the mid-1990s, focusing entirely on the native grape. Their basic bottling established Agrigento-area Nero d'Avola as a distinct expression of the variety.

milestones.event: Morgante first bottles Nero d'Avola under estate label

wines.description: Morgante's estate Nero d'Avola from inland Agrigento: full-bodied, warm-fruited and approachable from a Nero-d'Avola-specialist producer.

wines.name: Morgante Don Antonio
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French barriques, 14-16 months

taste.summary: Don Antonio is Morgante's single-vineyard selection: the richest, most concentrated Nero d'Avola from the estate with barrique aging producing a wine of chocolate-dark-fruit depth and silky structure.

pairings.why: Dense dark fruit and firm tannin are well-matched to the richness of wild boar pasta.

pairings.dish: Grilled pork ribs with Sicilian herbs
pairings.why: Chocolate-dark-fruit profile complements heavily spiced and charred pork.
The wine's chocolate-espresso notes make it a rare red that actually works with dark chocolate dessert.

history.summary: Don Antonio was named for a family patriarch and represents Morgante's premium single-vineyard Nero d'Avola, aged in French barriques to produce a richer, more structured expression of the estate's best fruit. It became one of the first Nero d'Avola bottlings to achieve international critical recognition.

milestones.event: First Don Antonio single-vineyard Nero d'Avola from barrique aging

wines.description: Morgante's top single-vineyard Nero d'Avola: concentrated, chocolate-dark-fruited and barrique-aged, one of Sicily's most recognised Nero d'Avola benchmarks.

wines.name: Zisola Doppiozeta
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French barriques and large Slavonian casks, 14 months

taste.summary: Doppiozeta is Zisola's flagship Nero d'Avola from Noto DOC: Mazzei's Tuscan winemaking precision applied to Sicilian Nero d'Avola produces a wine of elegant structure and tobacco-mineral depth.

pairings.why: Firm structured tannin from Mazzei's Tuscan style finds harmony with char-grilled beef.
Tobacco and dark fruit notes complement the richness of the region's indigenous pork breed.
Mineral depth and tobacco character pair well with this prized aged Italian cheese.

history.summary: The Mazzei family from Tuscany established Zisola near Noto in 2003, and by 2005 produced the first Doppiozeta as their top-tier single-estate Nero d'Avola. The double-Z name plays on the estate name and suggests the deepest expression of the Zisola terroir.

milestones.event: Mazzei family establishes Zisola estate near Noto
First Doppiozeta Noto DOC Nero d'Avola produced

wines.description: Zisola's flagship Noto DOC Nero d'Avola: Mazzei's precision winemaking applied to Sicilian Nero d'Avola from the Baroque-country Noto estate.

wines.name: Zisola
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French barriques, 12 months

taste.summary: The estate red blends Nero d'Avola's dark fruit with Syrah's peppery spice, delivering a richer, more internationally styled Sicilian red with smooth tannin and warmth.

pairings.why: Nero d'Avola dark fruit and Syrah pepper are the classic companions for grilled herb-spiced lamb skewers.
Medium tannin and smooth body are well matched to baked pasta with aubergine.
Warm dark fruit and spice pair comfortably with this island's most characteristic aged cheese.

history.summary: Zisola is the estate entry-level blend, producing a more accessible style than the Doppiozeta flagship by incorporating Syrah for a peppery-spice lift to the Nero d'Avola core.

milestones.event: First Zisola estate blend produced alongside Doppiozeta

wines.description: Zisola estate red blending Nero d'Avola and Syrah from the Noto DOC: warm, dark-fruited and peppery-spiced from the Mazzei family's Sicilian estate.

wines.name: Zisola Mofete
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel only

taste.summary: Named for local volcanic-spring activity, Mofete is Zisola's fresh Grillo: peach-citrus aromatics, herbal freshness and clean mineral finish from the warm Noto terroir.

pairings.dish: Grilled prawns with garlic and lemon
pairings.why: Citrus blossom and fresh mineral notes complement chargrilled shellfish perfectly.
Peach and herb aromatics bridge mozzarella, ripe tomato and basil.
High acid and salted-herb notes complement the brininess of capers against raw tuna.

history.summary: Mofete, meaning volcanic gas vents, is Zisola's white wine addition, using Grillo from the sun-warmed Noto soils. It reflects the Mazzei family's commitment to native Sicilian whites alongside their red focus.

milestones.event: First Mofete Grillo produced from Noto-area estate vineyards

wines.description: Zisola's Grillo white from the Noto DOC: fresh, peach-citrus and herbal, an aromatic native-white from the Mazzei family's Sicilian estate.

wines.name: Planeta Cerasuolo di Vittoria
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large Slavonian oak casks, 10-12 months

taste.summary: Planeta's DOCG expression from the Dorilli contrada in Vittoria: classic cherry-sour-herb character of the appellation with the polish and consistency Planeta's winemaking team delivers across all estates.

pairings.why: The wine's Frappato acidity and Nero d'Avola warmth complement the herb-stuffed pork.
Cerasuolo di Vittoria's relative lightness and acidity make it one of the few reds suited to grilled tuna.
Bright red-fruit acidity provides refreshing contrast to the richness of fried stuffed rice.

history.summary: Planeta established their Vittoria estate in the Dorilli contrada in 1997, one of the first major Sicilian producers to recognise the potential of the Cerasuolo di Vittoria appellation. Their DOCG bottling helped raise the profile of the zone internationally.

milestones.event: Planeta establishes Dorilli estate in Vittoria, first DOCG production
Cerasuolo di Vittoria elevated to DOCG status

wines.description: Planeta's Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG from the Dorilli contrada: classic cherry-herb character of Sicily's only DOCG with Planeta's characteristic polish.

wines.name: Planeta Frappato
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel and neutral wood, 8 months

taste.summary: Planeta's Frappato from Vittoria is an accessible, fresh expression of the variety: raspberry and rose aromas, light body and high acidity make it one of Sicily's most versatile lighter reds.

pairings.dish: Pizza bianca with ricotta and vegetables
pairings.why: Light body, high acid and floral notes are ideal companions for pizza without tomato.
The lightest of reds, Frappato is unusual in working alongside white fish.
Fresh red-berry and floral notes complement the cool creaminess of burrata.

history.summary: Planeta added a varietal Frappato from the Dorilli estate to showcase the grape's individual character, drawing on the same Vittoria soils as their DOCG blend but in a purer, unoaked expression.

milestones.event: First varietal Frappato from Planeta's Dorilli Vittoria estate

wines.description: Planeta's fresh and approachable varietal Frappato from the Vittoria DOC: light, floral, raspberry-fresh.

wines.name: Maggio Vini Nero d'Avola
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large oak casks, 12 months

taste.summary: Maggio Vini's Nero d'Avola from the ultra-sun-drenched Maccari-Noto area offers one of the island's richest and warmest expressions of the variety: full-bodied, carob-dark-fruited with licorice depth.

pairings.why: The warmth and full body of Noto Nero d'Avola are made for slow-cooked, richly caramelised pork.
Dense dark fruit and medium tannin support gelatinous braised cuts.
Carob and dark-chocolate flavour in the wine echoes sweet dark chocolate.

history.summary: Maggio Vini produce Noto DOC Nero d'Avola from the Maccari contrada near Syracuse, one of the hottest wine zones in Italy. The estate focus on native Sicilian grapes in one of the variety's heartland areas.

milestones.event: First Maggio Vini Noto DOC Nero d'Avola bottled from Maccari

wines.description: Maggio Vini's Noto DOC Nero d'Avola from the sun-drenched Maccari contrada near Syracuse: full-bodied, carob-dark-fruited and warm.

wines.name: Donnafugata Mille e Una Notte
wines.oak_regime: Aged in small French oak barriques, 14-16 months

taste.summary: Donnafugata's flagship red, Mille e Una Notte blends Nero d'Avola with Petit Verdot and Syrah from the Contessa Entellina estate: concentrated, structured and age-worthy with exceptional depth.

pairings.dish: Slow-roasted leg of lamb with aromatic herbs
pairings.why: Firm tannin and dark fruit concentration match the richness of a whole roasted lamb.
Dense dark chocolate notes and mineral depth complement earthy game and mushroom.
The island's greatest aged cheese finds a worthy partner in the island's most celebrated red.

history.summary: Donnafugata created Mille e Una Notte in 1997 as their prestige red from the Contessa Entellina estate in the Sicilian hinterland. The name references the Arabian Nights and evokes Sicily's Arab heritage; the wine is now the standard-bearer for Sicilian reds in international fine-wine circles.

milestones.event: First Mille e Una Notte produced from Contessa Entellina estate

wines.description: Donnafugata's prestige Contessa Entellina DOC blend led by Nero d'Avola: structured, concentrated and among Sicily's most age-worthy reds.

wines.name: Donnafugata Sherazade
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large Slavonian oak casks and stainless steel

taste.summary: Donnafugata's entry-level Nero d'Avola offers the grape's warm, plum-cherry character with the smooth, approachable style the estate is known for at this price point.

pairings.why: A reliable everyday Sicilian red that suits tomato-based meat pasta dishes.
Medium tannin and warm fruit complement charred pork sausage with roast peppers.
Smooth medium-bodied style works with fresh-to-semi-aged sheep's-milk cheeses.

history.summary: Sherazade was launched by Donnafugata as an accessible varietal Nero d'Avola to complement the Mille e Una Notte flagship. Like the flagship, the name references the Arabian Nights storytelling tradition of Sicily's Arab heritage.

milestones.event: First Sherazade varietal Nero d'Avola launched as estate entry red

wines.description: Donnafugata's approachable varietal Nero d'Avola: warm, plum-cherry, smooth and food-friendly from the Rallo family's Sicilian estate.

wines.name: Donnafugata Tancredi
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French barriques, 14 months

taste.summary: Tancredi blends the warmth of Nero d'Avola with Cabernet Sauvignon's structural backbone, producing a Sicilian-Bordeaux hybrid with cedar-cedar depth and concentrated fruit aged gracefully in French barriques.

pairings.why: The Bordeaux structure in Tancredi brings out the richness of island pork.
Cedar and tobacco notes complement white meat braised with aromatic herbs.
Structured tannin and dark fruit meet the sharpness of aged cow's-milk cheese.

history.summary: Tancredi was one of the earliest Sicilian wines to blend a native grape (Nero d'Avola) with an international variety (Cabernet Sauvignon). Named for the Visconti film set in Sicily, it predates the Super Tuscan movement's influence on the south and remains Donnafugata's most enduring mid-tier red.

milestones.event: First Tancredi blend from Contessa Entellina estate

wines.description: Donnafugata's Nero d'Avola and Cabernet Sauvignon blend from Contessa Entellina: Sicilian warmth with Bordeaux structure, first produced in 1990.

wines.name: Planeta Santa Cecilia
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French barriques, 12 months

taste.summary: Santa Cecilia is Planeta's prestige single-estate Nero d'Avola from Noto: rich, structured and mineral with chocolate-dark-fruit depth and the long finish that defines this appellation.

pairings.dish: Grilled aged beef tagliata with rocket
pairings.why: Concentrated dark fruit and firm tannin are the natural companions to dry-aged beef.
The wine's Noto heat and long finish match the richness of hours-slow-braised lamb.
The chocolate register in the wine makes this a bold dessert pairing.

history.summary: Planeta launched Santa Cecilia in 1997 from their Noto estate, drawing on the historic heartland of Nero d'Avola cultivation in south-east Sicily. It was among the wines that established Planeta as one of Italy's leading producers and Sicily as a serious fine-wine region.

milestones.event: First Santa Cecilia produced from Noto Nero d'Avola

wines.description: Planeta's prestige Noto DOC Nero d'Avola: concentrated, structured, chocolate-mineral and among Sicily's most internationally recognised reds.

wines.name: Duca di Salaparuta Duca Enrico
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French barriques, 12-14 months

taste.summary: Duca Enrico was Sicily's first landmark 100% Nero d'Avola: barrique-aged, structured and concentrated, it defined the modern Sicilian red wine archetype and remains a pillar of the island's wine history.

pairings.why: One of Sicily's oldest Nero d'Avola landmarks pairs naturally with the island's classic roast lamb.
Firm tannin and concentrated dark fruit stand up to collagen-rich slow-cooked beef cuts.
Tobacco and espresso notes complement the sharp crystalline character of aged Ragusano.

history.summary: Duca Enrico was launched in 1984 as the first serious premium 100% Nero d'Avola bottled in Sicily. Before this wine, Nero d'Avola was used primarily for blending. Duca di Salaparuta named it for the title of the founding family and it opened the door to the Nero d'Avola revolution of the 1990s and 2000s.

milestones.event: First Duca Enrico: Sicily's first premium 100% Nero d'Avola
Duca di Salaparuta acquired by Illva Saronno; production continues under same label

wines.description: Sicily's original prestige Nero d'Avola, first produced in 1984: the wine that opened the island's red-wine renaissance and remains a landmark bottling.

wines.name: Baglio del Cristo di Campobello Lu Patri
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French and Slavonian oak casks, 14 months

taste.summary: Lu Patri is Baglio del Cristo's prestige Nero d'Avola from chalky limestone soils near Agrigento: opulent dark fruit, chocolate and mineral depth from one of the island's most acclaimed organic Nero d'Avola producers.

pairings.why: Firm tannin and concentrated fruit meet the intensity of slow-cooked herb-seasoned lamb.
The wine's chocolate depth and firm structure are excellent partners for well-aged Sicilian cow's-milk cheese.
Earthy mineral notes in the wine complement the forest mushroom earthiness.

history.summary: The Bonetta family founded Baglio del Cristo di Campobello in 2000, and Lu Patri quickly emerged as their prestige expression. The Sicilian dialect name translates as 'the father' and represents the estate's oldest and finest Nero d'Avola fruit from chalky-limestone calcareous soils.

milestones.event: Baglio del Cristo di Campobello founded by the Bonetta family
First Lu Patri prestige Nero d'Avola bottled from oldest estate vines

wines.description: Baglio del Cristo di Campobello's prestige organic Nero d'Avola: concentrated, chocolate-mineral and opulent from chalky Agrigento limestone soils.

verified.address_quoted: Contrada Favarotta S.S. 123 km 19+200, 92023 Campobello di Licata (AG)

wines.name: Feudi del Pisciotto Cerasuolo di Vittoria
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large Slavonian oak casks, 10 months

taste.summary: Feudi del Pisciotto's DOCG expression from the restored manor estate in the Val di Noto shows the Cerasuolo di Vittoria character cleanly: cherry, mineral and herbaceous with the appellation's characteristic clay-soil earthiness.

pairings.why: Classic Cerasuolo pairing: the wine's herbal mineral notes echo the fennel while Nero d'Avola holds the pork.
Lighter red meat suits the medium-bodied, herb-inflected DOCG style.
High acidity and herbal character complement white-meat pasta sauces.

history.summary: Feudi del Pisciotto is set in an 18th-century restored manor on the edge of the Sughereta di Niscemi Nature Reserve. The estate produces Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG alongside a range of Sicilian varietals, combining wine production with boutique hotel and restaurant offerings.

milestones.event: First Cerasuolo di Vittoria produced from Feudi del Pisciotto estate

wines.description: Feudi del Pisciotto's Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG from the restored 18th-century Val di Noto manor estate: classic cherry-herb-mineral DOCG character.

wines.name: Feudi del Pisciotto Versace Nero d'Avola
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French barriques, 12 months

taste.summary: The Versace-labelled Nero d'Avola from Feudi del Pisciotto is part of the estate's designer fashion-house label series; the wine is a full, smooth and polished Nero d'Avola of immediate approachability.

pairings.dish: Pizza with grilled vegetables and mozzarella
pairings.why: Smooth medium tannin and warm dark fruit work with a range of pizza toppings.

pairings.dish: Grilled beef burger with aged cheese
pairings.why: Full body and vanilla notes from barriques complement a well-seasoned beef burger.
An everyday full-bodied Sicilian red suited to classic pasta dishes.

history.summary: Feudi del Pisciotto launched a series of label collaborations with Italian fashion houses including Versace, Missoni and Dolce and Gabbana. Each house designed a label for a varietal Sicilian wine. The Versace label is applied to the estate's principal Nero d'Avola.

milestones.event: First Versace-labelled Nero d'Avola launched as part of the fashion collaboration series

wines.description: Feudi del Pisciotto's Versace-labelled Nero d'Avola: smooth, full-bodied and polished, part of the estate's Italian fashion-house label series.

wines.name: Tasca d'Almerita Rosso del Conte
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large Slavonian oak casks, 24 months

taste.summary: Sicily's most historic prestige red: Tasca's Rosso del Conte blends Nero d'Avola with Perricone from the Regaleali plateau, aged two years in large oak. It is the island's longest-established benchmark for structured, age-worthy Sicilian reds.

pairings.why: Only a wine of this structure can stand up to a whole slow-roasted lamb at the Regaleali estate.
High tannin and acidity are the classic structural match for the concentrated aged-cheese board.
The wine's tobacco-mineral depth and dense fruit complement game with earthy accompaniments.

history.summary: Rosso del Conte has been produced at Regaleali since 1970, making it Sicily's oldest prestige estate red. Lucio Tasca d'Almerita created it when Sicilian fine wine barely existed as a concept, drawing on the plateau's native Nero d'Avola and the nearly extinct Perricone grape. It remains the definitive expression of Regaleali terroir.

milestones.event: First Rosso del Conte produced at Regaleali; Sicily's first modern estate red

wines.description: Sicily's most historic prestige estate red, produced since 1970 at Tasca d'Almerita's Regaleali plateau: structured, tobacco-mineral and among Italy's most age-worthy southern reds.

wines.name: Tasca d'Almerita Regaleali Bianco
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; cold-fermented to preserve aromatics

taste.summary: Sicily's most widely distributed native-white blend: Regaleali Bianco is a reliable, aromatic white of citrus and stone fruit freshness from the island's historic Tasca estate.

pairings.why: Refreshing acidity and citrus notes complement simply grilled Mediterranean fish.
A Sicilian picnic staple: Regaleali Bianco is the house white for seafood antipasto.
Clean mineral and citrus profile work well with cream-filled fried rice balls.

history.summary: Regaleali Bianco is one of Sicily's oldest and most widely recognised white wines, produced at the Tasca d'Almerita estate since the mid-1960s. It pioneered the idea of quality Sicilian dry white wine at a time when the island was known mainly for bulk production.

milestones.event: First Regaleali Bianco produced from estate native white varietals

wines.description: Tasca d'Almerita's classic native-white blend: Inzolia, Catarratto and Grecanico from Regaleali, fresh, citrus-mineral and among Sicily's best-known whites.

wines.name: Tasca d'Almerita Nozze d'Oro
wines.oak_regime: Fermented in stainless steel; partial aging on lees

taste.summary: Created for the fiftieth anniversary of Lucio and Franca Tasca's wedding, Nozze d'Oro pairs Inzolia's stone-fruit softness with Sauvignon Blanc's aromatic herbal freshness in an enduring premium Sicilian white.

pairings.why: Mineral acidity and elderflower lift are classic matches for clams in white wine sauce.
The wine's herbal freshness and stone fruit complement delicate pan-fried flatfish.
Herbal acidity bridges the sweet-sour aubergine-caper relish of caponata.

history.summary: Tasca d'Almerita created Nozze d'Oro in 1980 to mark the golden wedding anniversary of Lucio and Franca Tasca d'Almerita. Blending the native Inzolia with Sauvignon Blanc was innovative for Sicily at the time and the wine has remained one of the estate's most recognisable whites.

milestones.event: First Nozze d'Oro created for the Tasca golden anniversary; Inzolia-Sauvignon Blanc blend

wines.description: Tasca d'Almerita's Inzolia and Sauvignon Blanc blend, created in 1980 for the family's golden wedding: aromatic, mineral and among Sicily's most enduring premium whites.

wines.name: Tasca d'Almerita Chardonnay
wines.oak_regime: Partially fermented in French barriques, with lees aging

taste.summary: Tasca's Chardonnay from the high Regaleali plateau shows what Sicilian sun and altitude do to Chardonnay: richer and more tropical than Burgundy but retaining the Regaleali site's mineral backbone.

pairings.why: The creamy, rich Chardonnay is the classic partner for lobster in butter.
Creamy texture and stone-fruit notes complement the delicate seafood risotto.
Full body and vanilla notes match the richness of a cream-based chicken dish.

history.summary: Tasca d'Almerita planted Chardonnay at Regaleali in the early 1980s, recognising that the plateau's altitude could produce a more restrained style than the coastal Sicilian heat. The wine proved that Chardonnay could thrive in Sicily's interior without losing complexity.

milestones.event: First Tasca Chardonnay from Regaleali at the introduction of international varietals to the estate

wines.description: Tasca d'Almerita's high-altitude Regaleali Chardonnay: richer and more tropical than European benchmarks but retaining the Sicilian plateau's mineral character.

wines.name: Tasca d'Almerita Leone d'Almerita
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel only

taste.summary: Leone d'Almerita is the everyday entry point of the Tasca range: Catarratto and Grillo in a fresh, clean aromatic white for versatile food matching.

pairings.why: An everyday Sicilian white perfectly suited to fried seafood.
Clean citrus profile and fresh acidity work across a range of seafood starters.
A gentle, refreshing white that suits summer salad lunches.

history.summary: Leone d'Almerita is Tasca's volume-entry white, using Catarratto and Grillo as the island's most widely planted native whites. It is the accessible face of the Regaleali estate for everyday drinking.

milestones.event: Leone d'Almerita launched as Tasca's entry-level native-white blend

wines.description: Tasca d'Almerita's everyday Catarratto-Grillo blend: fresh, clean and aromatic, the accessible white wine from the historic Regaleali estate.

wines.name: Tasca d'Almerita Regaleali Rosso
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large Slavonian oak casks, 10 months

taste.summary: Regaleali Rosso has been Tasca's everyday red since 1968: a multi-varietal native blend of Nero d'Avola, Perricone and Nerello Mascalese showing the breadth of the Regaleali plateau's terroir.

pairings.why: An everyday Sicilian red that suits pork sausage pasta reliably.
Medium body and herbal notes pair comfortably with roast chicken.
Warm red fruit and medium tannin hold their own against the sweet-sour intensity of caponata.

history.summary: Regaleali Rosso is one of Sicily's longest-running estate reds, predating the island's fine-wine revolution. It uses native varieties from the Regaleali plateau and has always been the workhorse red of the Tasca range.

milestones.event: First Regaleali Rosso produced from estate native red varietals

wines.description: Tasca d'Almerita's classic multi-varietal Sicilian red, produced since 1968: Nero d'Avola, Perricone and Nerello Mascalese in an approachable, food-friendly style.

wines.name: Donnafugata Anthilia
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; cold fermentation at 16-18 degrees

taste.summary: Anthilia is Donnafugata's flagship entry white: Catarratto and Grecanico from the Contessa Entellina estate in a fresh, floral and mineral style that has defined quality Sicilian white wine for three decades.

pairings.why: Clean acidity and citrus notes are the natural match for garlicky prawns.
A Sicilian classic: Anthilia's freshness works seamlessly with marinated seafood.
Light floral aromatics and fresh acidity complement the mild, herby ricotta.

history.summary: Donnafugata launched Anthilia in 1990 as their signature fresh white from the Contessa Entellina estate. The name was inspired by the Nymphaeum at Selinunte, a classical Sicilian archaeological site. It has become one of the island's most widely distributed quality whites.

milestones.event: First Anthilia from Contessa Entellina Catarratto and Grecanico

wines.description: Donnafugata's signature native white: Catarratto and Grecanico from Contessa Entellina, fresh, floral and mineral, one of Sicily's most widely recognised whites.

wines.name: Donnafugata Lighea
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel only

taste.summary: Lighea blends Zibibbo's heady Muscat-like perfume with native white grapes in a delicately off-dry aromatic style: floral, peachy, with balancing acidity that keeps it from cloying.

pairings.why: The wine's aromatic floral sweetness complements herb-crusted salmon delicately.
The off-dry aromatic style is unusually suited to lighter Asian preparations.
The hint of sweetness and floral character bridges fresh fruit and soft ripened cheese.

history.summary: Lighea was created by Donnafugata in 1992 to explore the aromatic potential of Zibibbo (Moscato d'Alessandria), blending it with native whites for balance. Named for a water nymph from a Tomasi di Lampedusa story, the wine has a distinct island identity.

milestones.event: First Lighea produced as Donnafugata's aromatic off-dry white

wines.description: Donnafugata's aromatic off-dry blend led by Zibibbo: floral, peachy and delicately sweet, named for a Lampedusa water nymph.

wines.name: Donnafugata Vigna di Gabri
wines.oak_regime: Aged partially on fine lees in stainless steel

taste.summary: Vigna di Gabri is Donnafugata's premium Contessa Entellina white: a blend of native Sicilian whites with Chardonnay, showing greater textural complexity than Anthilia with a more persistent mineral finish.

pairings.why: Round stone fruit and mineral acidity are an ideal match for prawn and courgette pasta.
Mineral depth and stone-fruit texture complement the clean flavour of whole grilled sea bass.
Herbal mineral notes in the wine bridge the creaminess of burrata and the nuttiness of artichoke.

history.summary: Vigna di Gabri was named for Gabriella Anca Rallo, wife of Giacomo Rallo and co-founder of Donnafugata. It represents the premium white cuvee from the Contessa Entellina estate, using the best parcels of native whites blended with Chardonnay for added structure.

milestones.event: First Vigna di Gabri; named for Gabriella Anca Rallo of the founding family

wines.description: Donnafugata's premium Contessa Entellina white: native Sicilian whites blended with Chardonnay for added texture and mineral complexity.

wines.name: Planeta La Segreta Bianco
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel only

taste.summary: La Segreta Bianco blends native Grecanico with aromatic international varieties in Planeta's most accessible white: fresh, aromatic and immediately appealing with stone-fruit and citrus freshness.

pairings.why: Acacia blossom and fresh citrus notes contrast pleasantly with fried mozzarella.
An everyday Sicilian white well suited to fresh clam or mussel pasta.
Clean acidity and aromatic freshness complement delicate raw fish preparations.

history.summary: La Segreta Bianco and Rosso were created by Planeta to offer approachable, multi-varietal introductions to Sicilian wine at an accessible price. The white blend was designed to combine Grecanico's native Sicilian character with the aromatics of Chardonnay, Viognier and Fiano.

milestones.event: La Segreta Bianco launched as Planeta's entry-level multi-varietal white

wines.description: Planeta's entry-level multi-varietal white blending Grecanico, Chardonnay, Viognier and Fiano: fresh, aromatic and widely approachable.

wines.name: Planeta Cometa
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel with lees aging, 6-8 months

taste.summary: Cometa is Sicily's signature Fiano: Planeta introduced Campanian Fiano to Menfi soils and produced a wine distinct from Southern Italian Fiano with a mineral-salt and almond-toasted character specific to the Mediterranean coast.

pairings.dish: Grilled cuttlefish with almonds and mint
pairings.why: The wine's almond-mineral notes are a classical match for grilled cephalopods with nut dressings.
Honeysuckle and stone fruit in the wine complement Sicilian pistachio pesto.
Full body and mineral salinity support the delicate sweetness of langoustines.

history.summary: Planeta's Cometa from Fiano, planted at Ulmo in Menfi in the early 1990s, became a landmark Sicilian white and helped establish Fiano as a versatile variety beyond its Campanian homeland. The 1998 first vintage won multiple international awards and established Menfi Fiano as a distinct sub-style.

milestones.event: First Cometa from Fiano vines at Menfi; immediate critical recognition

wines.description: Planeta's benchmark Fiano from Menfi DOC: toasted-almond, honeysuckle and mineral-salt, Sicily's definitive expression of this Campanian variety.

wines.name: Planeta Chardonnay
wines.oak_regime: Fermented and aged in French barriques (50% new), 8-9 months with batonnage

taste.summary: Planeta's barrique-aged Chardonnay from Ulmo at Menfi became one of the wines that announced Sicily's quality transformation in the 1990s: rich, buttery, confident, with Sicilian sunshine layered through Burgundian oak treatment.

pairings.why: Rich creamy oak treatment is the benchmark match for cream-sauce lobster.
Full body and buttery notes complement rich cream-based poultry dishes.
Hazelnut-vanilla notes from barrique aging pair with the sweetness of pan-seared scallops.

history.summary: Planeta's Chardonnay was one of the earliest premium barrique-aged whites from Sicily, first produced in 1994. It was instrumental in changing perceptions of Sicilian white wine quality and became one of Italy's most internationally sold premium whites in the late 1990s and 2000s.

milestones.event: First Planeta Chardonnay from Menfi; one of Sicily's earliest barrique-aged whites

wines.description: Planeta's landmark barrique-aged Chardonnay from Menfi, first produced in 1994: rich, creamy and the wine that announced Sicily's quality white-wine transformation.

wines.name: Cusumano Angimbe
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel for Inzolia; partial barrique for Chardonnay component

taste.summary: Cusumano's Inzolia-Chardonnay blend is an accessible, well-made Sicilian white that combines the native grape's almond-citrus freshness with Chardonnay's stone-fruit roundness.

pairings.why: Clean acidity and almond notes complement the oil and richness of fried sardines.
A reliable everyday Sicilian white for tuna pasta dishes.
Medium body and citrus notes work well with straightforward grilled poultry.

history.summary: Cusumano created Angimbe as their entry-level white blend, using Inzolia grown at their estates and Chardonnay for balance and appeal. Named for the Sicilian word for fragrant.

milestones.event: First Angimbe from Inzolia and Chardonnay at Cusumano

wines.description: Cusumano's approachable Inzolia-Chardonnay blend: fresh, citrus-almond and an everyday Sicilian white from the Partinico estate.

verified.address_quoted: SS 113 Km 307, Contrada San Carlo, 90047 Partinico (PA)

wines.name: Cusumano Jale
wines.oak_regime: Fermented and aged in French barriques (new and second-fill), 8 months

taste.summary: Cusumano's premium Chardonnay, Jale, shows the full-sun ripeness of Sicilian Chardonnay through barrique treatment: rich, creamy and tropical with hazelnut-vanilla depth.

pairings.why: Rich body and creamy oak notes complement cream-sauced seafood pasta.

pairings.dish: Veal medallions with butter and sage
pairings.why: Full Chardonnay body works with the richness of butter-sage veal.
Vanilla-tropical notes in the wine pair with the honey-sweetness of brie or camembert.

history.summary: Jale is Cusumano's flagship Chardonnay, drawing on their best Chardonnay vineyard parcels and given full barrique treatment. The name evokes the Sicilian coastal landscape.

milestones.event: First Jale Chardonnay from Cusumano's premium estate selection

wines.description: Cusumano's premium barrique-aged Chardonnay: rich, creamy and tropical from the Sicilian interior's sun-warmed vineyards.

verified.address_quoted: SS 113 Km 307, Contrada San Carlo, 90047 Partinico (PA)

wines.name: Cusumano Benuara
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French barriques, 12 months

taste.summary: Cusumano's Nero d'Avola and Syrah blend from Sicilian estate vineyards: the pepper-spice of Syrah amplifies Nero d'Avola's dark fruit and the barrique treatment adds structure and warmth.

pairings.why: Syrah pepper and Nero d'Avola dark fruit match the herby, slightly gamey flavour of lamb.

pairings.dish: Pasta with meat ragu and chilli
pairings.why: Full body and pepper-spice hold up to robust, chilli-spiked meat sauces.
Firm tannin and warm mineral depth match the sharp intensity of aged provolone.

history.summary: Benuara was Cusumano's mid-tier blend, combining the native Nero d'Avola with Syrah for international appeal. It was part of the estate's strategy to create a full range from entry to premium, covering both native and international varieties.

milestones.event: First Benuara Nero d'Avola-Syrah blend produced

wines.description: Cusumano's Nero d'Avola and Syrah blend: full-bodied, peppery-spiced and structured from the Partinico estate.

verified.address_quoted: SS 113 Km 307, Contrada San Carlo, 90047 Partinico (PA)

wines.name: Cusumano Sagana
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French barriques (new and second-fill), 14 months

taste.summary: Sagana is Cusumano's prestige single-vineyard Nero d'Avola from Riesi in central Sicily: opulent, concentrated and barrique-treated to produce a richly chocolate-dark wine of considerable structure.

pairings.why: The opulence and structure of Sagana are matched by the richness of slow-cooked lamb.
Rich chocolate-coffee notes complement the spiced cured-meat character of Sicilian salami.
Only the most structured and concentrated red wines can stand up to dry-aged ribeye.

history.summary: Sagana is Cusumano's single-vineyard Nero d'Avola from a specific Riesi parcel in the Caltanissetta province where the estate owns one of its best vineyards. It was designed from the outset as the flagship of the Cusumano range.

milestones.event: First Sagana from the Riesi single-vineyard Nero d'Avola

wines.description: Cusumano's prestige single-vineyard Nero d'Avola from Riesi: opulent, chocolate-cedar and among the fullest expressions of the variety.

verified.address_quoted: SS 113 Km 307, Contrada San Carlo, 90047 Partinico (PA)

wines.name: Mandrarossa Terre di Giumara Fiano
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; cold fermentation

taste.summary: Mandrarossa's single-vineyard Fiano from the Terre di Giumara estate shows the variety's characteristic nutty-mineral quality from Menfi's calcareous-clay soils close to the Mediterranean coast.

pairings.why: Almond-mineral notes and high acidity complement deep-fried squid with garlic sauce.

pairings.dish: Pasta con le sarde and fennel
pairings.why: Fiano's nutty mineral register works with fennel and sardine pasta.
Stone-fruit richness and mineral acidity are the natural Fiano pairing for grilled shellfish.

history.summary: Mandrarossa was created in 1999 as the premium selection label of Cantine Settesoli, focusing on single-vineyard expressions from the cooperative's best Menfi terroir plots. Terre di Giumara is their flagship Fiano, anchored on a specific coastal estate.

milestones.event: Mandrarossa premium label launched from Settesoli cooperative
First Terre di Giumara Fiano released as single-vineyard premium white

wines.description: Mandrarossa's single-vineyard Fiano from Menfi DOC: almond-mineral, stone-fruit and with nutty persistence from coastal calcareous-clay soils.

wines.name: Mandrarossa Timperosse
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French barriques, 14 months

taste.summary: Mandrarossa's Timperosse is one of the rare 100% Petit Verdot bottlings outside Bordeaux: in Sicily's heat the variety reaches complete ripeness, producing an inky, dense and powerfully structured wine with extraordinary colour and longevity.

pairings.why: The wine's power and tannin demand the most collagen-rich and long-cooked cuts.
High tannin and dense fruit are the structural match for intensely aged hard cheese.
Inky dark fruit and violet notes complement earthy truffle and forest mushroom.

history.summary: Mandrarossa's Timperosse from 100% Petit Verdot was a pioneering experiment in growing this Bordeaux variety in Menfi. Its success inspired other Sicilian estates and it remains Italy's most recognised varietal Petit Verdot.

milestones.event: First Timperosse from 100% Petit Verdot; one of Italy's first varietal Petit Verdot bottlings

wines.description: Mandrarossa's 100% Petit Verdot from Menfi DOC: inky, dense and powerfully structured, Italy's benchmark varietal Petit Verdot.

wines.name: Mandrarossa Nero d'Avola
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large Slavonian oak casks, 10 months

taste.summary: Mandrarossa's varietal Nero d'Avola shows the cooperative's Menfi terroir at its most accessible: cherry-plum, herbal and warm, a reliable everyday Sicilian red with the typical coastal-influence freshness of Menfi.

pairings.why: Medium tannin and warm fruit pair naturally with spicy cured-meat pizza.

pairings.dish: Pasta al sugo di pomodoro fresco
pairings.why: A simple, reliable Sicilian red that suits fresh tomato-based pasta.
Warm Nero d'Avola character finds its natural match in grilled Sicilian sausage.

history.summary: Mandrarossa's Nero d'Avola was one of the label's foundation wines, launched in 1999 to showcase Menfi's warm growing conditions for the grape. It helped establish the Settesoli cooperative as a quality player in the Nero d'Avola category.

milestones.event: First Mandrarossa Nero d'Avola from Menfi cooperative terroir

wines.description: Mandrarossa's everyday Menfi DOC Nero d'Avola: cherry-plum, herbal and warm from the Settesoli cooperative's large Menfi estate.

wines.name: Mandrarossa Grillo
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel only

taste.summary: Mandrarossa's coastal Grillo from Menfi shows the grape's Mediterranean character: sun-warmed citrus blossom and peach with sea-salt mineral and high acidity from close proximity to the Tyrrhenian coast.

pairings.why: Citrus blossom and sea-salt mineral match the citrus herb sauce on grilled swordfish.
High acidity and coastal mineral notes work across a range of sea-fresh starters.
The wine's refreshing acidity and citrus brighten simple tomato-oil bread.

history.summary: Mandrarossa added a varietal Grillo to their range to offer the most characteristically Sicilian white experience at their price point. The Menfi coast's proximity to the sea gives this Grillo a salinity not found in inland bottlings.

milestones.event: First varietal Grillo from Mandrarossa coastal Menfi vineyards

wines.description: Mandrarossa's coastal Menfi DOC Grillo: citrus-blossom, sea-salt mineral and bright, from the Settesoli cooperative's Tyrrhenian coastal vineyards.

wines.name: Mandrarossa Catarratto
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel only

taste.summary: Mandrarossa's varietal Catarratto is the most characteristically Sicilian of the range: Sicily's most widely planted white variety in its simplest, freshest expression from Menfi's coastal estate.

pairings.why: The wine's almond-herb freshness is the classic Sicilian match for anchovy pasta.
Light, crisp and fresh: a summer salad wine for daytime meals.
Catarratto's clean acidity cleanses the oil from grilled whole sardines.

history.summary: Mandrarossa produces Catarratto as an affordable expression of Sicily's most widely grown grape, demonstrating that careful winemaking from the cooperative's Menfi estates can produce a clean, characterful varietal Catarratto.

milestones.event: First Mandrarossa varietal Catarratto from coastal Menfi vineyards

wines.description: Mandrarossa's everyday varietal Catarratto: light, almond-citrus and refreshing, the most accessible face of Sicily's most widely planted white grape.

wines.name: Rapitala Grand Cru
wines.oak_regime: Fermented and aged in new French barriques, 8-10 months with batonnage

taste.summary: Rapitala Grand Cru is the estate's flagship Chardonnay: a full barrique-fermented Sicilian Chardonnay from the Camporeale hillside estate combining tropical fruit richness with a toasty-creamy Burgundian framework.

pairings.why: The wine's creamy, toasty depth complements rich mushroom risotto.
Full-bodied barrique Chardonnay is the classical match for grilled lobster.
Vanilla-toast notes and rich body pair seamlessly with cream-based veal.

history.summary: Rapitala was established in the Sicilian hinterland of Camporeale in 1968 through a partnership between Count Hugues de la Gatinais and the Guarrasi family. Grand Cru was developed as their prestige Chardonnay, bearing the name influenced by the Burgundian classification despite having no official Italian Grand Cru status.

milestones.event: Tenuta Rapitala established by Franco-Sicilian partnership in Camporeale
Grand Cru Chardonnay developed as prestige flagship white

wines.description: Rapitala's prestige barrique-fermented Chardonnay from the Camporeale hillside: rich, creamy, tropical-fruited and toasty.

wines.name: Rapitala Hugonis
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French barriques (new and second-fill), 14 months

taste.summary: Hugonis, named for the founding de la Gatinais family, blends Cabernet Sauvignon's structure with Nero d'Avola's Sicilian warmth in a wine that reflects the estate's Franco-Sicilian origins.

pairings.why: Cabernet structure and Sicilian warmth are the natural companions for herb-encrusted lamb.
Cedar-tobacco depth and firm tannin are traditional partners for a Sicilian cheese selection.
Tobacco-cedar notes complement earthy mushroom pasta dishes.

history.summary: Hugonis was created as the Camporeale estate's prestige red, honouring the French founding partner Count Hugues de la Gatinais. The blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Nero d'Avola reflects the estate's Franco-Sicilian identity.

milestones.event: First Hugonis blending Cabernet Sauvignon and Nero d'Avola; named for founder Hugues

wines.description: Rapitala's prestige red blending Cabernet Sauvignon and Nero d'Avola: Franco-Sicilian in character, structured and cedar-mineral from the Camporeale hillside.

wines.name: Rapitala Catarratto
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; low-temperature fermentation

taste.summary: Rapitala's varietal Catarratto from the Alcamo DOC shows the variety's almond-citrus freshness from high-altitude Camporeale vines with a cleaner, more mineral profile than coastal examples.

pairings.why: Almond and citrus notes complement Sicilian-style tuna with caper-tomato sauce.
Catarratto's almond-herbal character suits cauliflower and anchovy pasta.
A Sicilian summer white wine for vegetable omelettes and light lunch dishes.

history.summary: Rapitala produces Catarratto from their Camporeale estate as a varietal expression of Sicily's most planted white grape in the Alcamo DOC, showcasing the variety's cleaner, more mineral potential from high-altitude inland vineyards.

milestones.event: First varietal Alcamo DOC Catarratto from the Rapitala Camporeale estate

wines.description: Rapitala's Alcamo DOC Catarratto: almond-citrus, mineral and fresh from high-altitude Camporeale vines.

wines.name: Baglio di Pianetto Ramione
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French barriques, 14 months

taste.summary: Ramione blends the dark fruit warmth of Nero d'Avola with the softening effect of Merlot at 900 metres above sea level: the result is a polished, smooth Sicilian blend with surprising freshness from altitude.

pairings.why: Smooth tannin and dark fruit match the richness of slow-braised bone-in beef.
Cedar and dark fruit notes complement earthy mushroom pasta.
Smooth warmth and plum notes pair nicely with sweet-salty aged sheep's milk cheese.

history.summary: Count Paolo Marzotto founded Baglio di Pianetto in 1997 and created the Ramione as his estate's prestige red blend, growing Nero d'Avola and Merlot at the unusually high altitude of 900 metres near Santa Cristina Gela.

milestones.event: Baglio di Pianetto founded by Count Paolo Marzotto
First Ramione blend from Nero d'Avola and Merlot at high-altitude estate

wines.description: Baglio di Pianetto's Nero d'Avola and Merlot blend from 900 metres altitude near Palermo: polished, dark-fruited and with surprising freshness for its weight.

verified.address_quoted: Via Francia, Santa Cristina Gela - Palermo, Italy

wines.name: Baglio di Pianetto Ficiligno
wines.oak_regime: Cold fermentation in stainless steel; no oak

taste.summary: Ficiligno blends Inzolia's almond-citrus freshness with Viognier's perfumed apricot-orange blossom character at high altitude near Palermo, producing an aromatic Sicilian white of unusual floral intensity.

pairings.dish: Grilled prawns with orange and herbs
pairings.why: The wine's orange blossom aromatics mirror the orange-citrus dressing and complement the shellfish.

pairings.dish: Salmone al forno con erbe aromatiche
pairings.why: Aromatic Viognier notes complement herb-baked salmon perfectly.
Floral stone-fruit aromatics bridge the sweet milkiness of buffalo mozzarella with ripe tomato.

history.summary: Baglio di Pianetto planted Viognier at their high-altitude estate to complement the native Inzolia, creating an unusually floral Sicilian white blend. Ficiligno is Sicilian dialect for fig tree, referencing the estate landscape.

milestones.event: First Ficiligno Inzolia-Viognier blend from the Pianetto altitude estate

wines.description: Baglio di Pianetto's aromatic Inzolia-Viognier blend: floral, stone-fruited and fresh from 900 metres altitude near Palermo.

verified.address_quoted: Via Francia, Santa Cristina Gela - Palermo, Italy

wines.name: Baglio di Pianetto Viafrancia
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French barriques, 14 months

taste.summary: Baglio di Pianetto's high-altitude Syrah shows how the variety performs at 900 metres: the elevation preserves aromatic freshness and pepper-spice that is often lost at lower-altitude Sicilian warmth.

pairings.why: Pepper-spice and dark fruit are the definitive Syrah pairing for herb-seasoned lamb.
Full body and peppery character stand up to gamey wild boar sausage.
Olive notes in the wine mirror the Provencal character of black olive tapenade.

history.summary: Baglio di Pianetto planted Syrah at their Pianetto estate and named the wine Viafrancia (Via Francia being the estate's street address), emphasising the French variety grown in a Sicilian context. High altitude preserves the variety's northern-Rhone pepper character.

milestones.event: First Viafrancia Syrah from the Pianetto high-altitude estate

wines.description: Baglio di Pianetto's high-altitude Sicilian Syrah: peppery, dark-fruited and mineral from 900 metres near Palermo.

verified.address_quoted: Via Francia, Santa Cristina Gela - Palermo, Italy

wines.name: Firriato Camelot
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French barriques (new and second-fill), 16 months

taste.summary: Firriato's Bordeaux-style blend from the Trapani hinterland: Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot grown in Sicily's sun produce a richly ripe interpretation of the classic Bordeaux blend with a warm cedar-tobacco finish.

pairings.why: A Bordeaux-style blend finds its classic pairing with char-grilled bone-in beef.
Dark fruit richness and firm tannin complement slow-braised collagen-rich pork.
Cedar and tobacco depth are structural matches for sharp aged sheep's-milk cheese.

history.summary: Firriato created Camelot in 1997 as their prestige Bordeaux-varietal blend, recognising that Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot grown in Sicily's warm western interior could produce an internationally styled wine. It became the flagship of their large multi-estate range.

milestones.event: First Camelot Cabernet-Merlot blend from Firriato Trapani estate

wines.description: Firriato's prestige Bordeaux-blend from western Sicily: Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot at their ripest, cedar-tobacco and structured.

wines.name: Firriato Ribeca
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French barriques, 12 months

taste.summary: Ribeca is one of Sicily's most important varietal Perricone wines: this nearly extinct native grape produces a wine of austere iron-mineral character, tart red fruit and high acidity that is unlike any other Sicilian red.

pairings.why: Perricone's pepper and iron mineral are classical with spice-seasoned lamb.
The wine's tartness and iron mineral complement the richness of blood sausage pasta.
High acidity and iron-mineral depth are structural matches for intensely aged cheese.

history.summary: Firriato produced Ribeca as a single-varietal Perricone to champion this near-extinct Sicilian native red grape. Perricone (also called Pignatello) was once widely grown in western Sicily but declined sharply in the 20th century. Firriato's bottling helped revive interest in the variety.

milestones.event: First Ribeca varietal Perricone produced; among the first modern single-varietal Perricone bottlings

wines.description: Firriato's varietal Perricone: a wine from a near-extinct Sicilian native grape, iron-mineral, tart and structurally unique among Sicilian reds.

wines.name: Firriato Chiaramonte Bianco
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; cold fermentation

taste.summary: Firriato's entry white blends the native almond-citrus character of Catarratto with Chardonnay's stone-fruit roundness in a clean, everyday Sicilian white from the Trapani estate.

pairings.why: Clean high acidity and citrus-mineral notes are the classic white for clam pasta.
A versatile everyday white for Mediterranean grilled fish.
Fresh and clean: the ideal house white for a varied vegetable starter.

history.summary: Chiaramonte is named for the historic Aragonese architectural style found throughout Sicily. Firriato's Chiaramonte Bianco is their accessible entry-level white from the Trapani estate's Catarratto and Chardonnay parcels.

milestones.event: First Chiaramonte Bianco from Catarratto and Chardonnay at Firriato

wines.description: Firriato's approachable Catarratto-Chardonnay blend: clean, citrus-mineral and everyday from the Trapani estate.

wines.name: Firriato Chiaramonte Nero d'Avola
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large Slavonian oak casks, 10 months

taste.summary: Firriato's entry-level Nero d'Avola under the Chiaramonte label is an accessible, fruit-forward expression of the variety: warm, rounded and designed for everyday enjoyment.

pairings.why: An everyday full-bodied Sicilian red for tomato-based meat pasta dishes.
Round medium tannin and warm fruit work with a range of classic pizza toppings.
A reliable everyday red for grilled Sicilian sausage at the barbecue.

history.summary: Firriato's Chiaramonte red uses Nero d'Avola from their large multi-estate range to produce an entry-level red of approachable everyday character. The Chiaramonte name spans their white and red entry-level range.

milestones.event: First Chiaramonte Nero d'Avola from Firriato's western Sicily estates

wines.description: Firriato's entry-level Nero d'Avola under the Chiaramonte label: warm, round and approachable for everyday Sicilian red wine drinking.

wines.name: Ajello Tajuni Catarratto
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; cold fermentation

taste.summary: Ajello's organic Catarratto from the Belice Valley near Castelvetrano offers a light, mineral expression of the variety: almond-blossom, lemon and chamomile with the clean freshness of organically farmed western Sicilian vines.

pairings.why: The wine's almond and lemon freshness are the natural match for Sicilian coastal sardines.

pairings.dish: Bruschetta with fresh tomatoes and basil
pairings.why: Light and clean: a summer aperitivo white for tomato-basil bread.
Delicate almond-floral notes pair with mild, fresh ricotta.

history.summary: Abele Ajello is a small organic producer in the Belice Valley near Castelvetrano, working with traditional Sicilian varieties. The Tajuni Catarratto is named in Sicilian dialect and represents the estate's signature organic white.

milestones.event: First Tajuni Catarratto from the organic Belice Valley estate

wines.description: Abele Ajello's organic Alcamo DOC Catarratto: light, almond-citrus and clean from the Belice Valley near Castelvetrano.

wines.name: Ajello Osa
wines.oak_regime: Aged in Slavonian oak, 10 months

taste.summary: Ajello's Osa red blend of Nero d'Avola and Grillo is an unusual pairing of a red and white variety in a co-fermented red: warm, herbal and distinct from straightforward varietal Nero d'Avola.

pairings.why: The wine's herbal character and warm fruit complement fresh herb-seasoned lamb.

pairings.dish: Pasta con le sarde in agrodolce
pairings.why: The unusual red-white co-ferment creates a versatile food-bridge red for this sweet-sour sardine pasta.
Medium tannin and herbal warmth suit the mild freshness of young sheep's milk cheese.

history.summary: Osa is Ajello's unusual co-fermented blend of Nero d'Avola and Grillo, exploring the western Sicilian tradition of blending red and white grapes. The organic farming approach reflects the estate's commitment to traditional methods.

milestones.event: First Osa co-fermented Nero d'Avola and Grillo blend from organic Belice Valley estate

wines.description: Abele Ajello's unusual co-fermented red: Nero d'Avola and Grillo blended, herbal, warm and distinctly western Sicilian in character.

wines.name: Tenuta Gatti Nero d'Avola
wines.oak_regime: Aged in Slavonian oak, 12 months

taste.summary: Tenuta Gatti's Nero d'Avola from the Nebrodi slopes in the Messina province is unusually mineral and herbal compared to southern Sicilian examples: the altitude and Nebrodi scrubland influence give a cooler, more aromatic character to this northerly Nero d'Avola.

pairings.dish: Grilled lamb with Sicilian wild herbs
pairings.why: The herbal mineral character matches the distinctive wild herb landscapes of the Nebrodi hills.

pairings.dish: Braised pork with fennel and olives
pairings.why: Dark fruit and Mediterranean herb character complement the classic Sicilian pork braise.
Mineral depth and dark fruit complement the character of cured island pork.

history.summary: Tenuta Gatti, a historic 217-hectare estate founded in 1825 at Librizzi in the Messina province, produces Nero d'Avola at an unusually northerly and high-altitude location. The Nebrodi slopes produce a distinctly different expression of the variety compared to the southern Sicilian heartland.

milestones.event: Tenuta Gatti estate founded at Librizzi in the Messina province
First modern bottling of Nero d'Avola under the Tenuta Gatti label

wines.description: Tenuta Gatti's northerly Messina-province Nero d'Avola: unusually herbal and mineral compared to southern examples, from historic 1825 Nebrodi slopes estate.

wines.name: Tenuta Gatti Pinot Nero
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French barriques, 10 months

taste.summary: Tenuta Gatti's Pinot Nero, grown at altitude on the Nebrodi slopes in northern Sicily, is one of Italy's southernmost Pinot Nero bottlings: light, mineral and earthy with the Nebrodi altitude moderating what would otherwise be an impossible growing zone for the variety.

pairings.why: Pinot Nero's delicacy and earthiness suit lean game birds.
Forest-floor earthiness and light tannin complement mushroom risotto naturally.
One of the few reds suited to grilled salmon due to its light body and high acidity.

history.summary: Tenuta Gatti cultivates Pinot Nero at altitude on the Nebrodi slopes, making it one of the very few estates in Sicily experimenting seriously with the variety. The cooler Nebrodi microclimate makes this unusual experiment viable and produces a distinctly Sicilian take on Pinot Nero.

milestones.event: First Tenuta Gatti Pinot Nero from Nebrodi altitude vineyards

wines.description: Tenuta Gatti's unusual Nebrodi altitude Pinot Nero: Italy's southernmost serious Pinot Nero, light, mineral and earthy from the historic Messina estate.

wines.name: Duca di Salaparuta Bianca di Valguarnera
wines.oak_regime: Fermented and aged partially in French barriques on lees, 8 months

taste.summary: Bianca di Valguarnera is Sicily's most celebrated Inzolia: Duca di Salaparuta's partial barrique treatment of the native grape produces a rich, nutty, mineral white with the depth to age several years.

pairings.why: Almond-nutty depth and stone-fruit richness complement prawn pasta with Sicilian pistachios.
The wine's almond character mirrors the nut-crusted fish in a natural aromatic echo.
Nutty mineral depth suits the mellow sharpness of fresh-to-aged tuma.

history.summary: Bianca di Valguarnera was created in 1985 as the prestige white of Duca di Salaparuta, named for the Principessa Enrica Valguarnera di Niscemi and representing the first serious attempt to produce a barrique-aged Inzolia in Sicily. It remains Sicily's benchmark single-varietal Inzolia.

milestones.event: First Bianca di Valguarnera; first barrique-aged Inzolia in Sicily

wines.description: Duca di Salaparuta's prestige Inzolia from Sicily: partially barrique-aged, nutty, mineral and Sicily's most celebrated single-varietal Inzolia.

wines.name: Planeta Noto Nero d'Avola
wines.oak_regime: Aged in Slavonian oak casks, 12 months

taste.summary: Planeta's Noto DOC Nero d'Avola from the Buonivini contrada captures the south-east of Sicily at its most sun-soaked: rich carob and dark cherry with the warm mineral finish typical of the Noto appellation's limestone soils.

pairings.why: Carob-warm Nero d'Avola is the ideal companion for rich pork braised with Sicilian olives.
Dark fruit and medium tannin hold up to the richness of sausage and brassica pasta.
The wine's warm mineral depth pairs well with the sharp crystalline character of aged Ragusano DOP.

history.summary: Planeta established the Buonivini estate near Noto in 2003 to explore what is considered the heartland of Nero d'Avola cultivation. The estate sits in the Contrada Buonivini within the Noto DOC, historically associated with the oldest and most valued Nero d'Avola vines.

milestones.event: Planeta establishes Buonivini estate in Noto; first Noto DOC Nero d'Avola

wines.description: Planeta's Noto DOC Nero d'Avola from the Buonivini contrada: warm, carob-dark-fruited and mineral from Sicily's Nero d'Avola heartland.

wines.name: Planeta Grillo
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; cold fermentation to preserve aromatics

taste.summary: Planeta's varietal Grillo from the Menfi estate shows the Mediterranean coastal character of the grape: citrus verbena, sea-salt mineral and herbal freshness with the clean precision of Planeta's winemaking.

pairings.why: Sea-salt mineral and citrus notes are the natural Grillo companions for whole grilled Mediterranean sea bass.
Herbal lift and citrus freshness complement the richness of Sicilian pistachio pesto.
Saline mineral character is a natural pairing for fresh oysters.

history.summary: Planeta began producing a varietal Grillo from Menfi to showcase the most characteristically Sicilian white grape in a pure expression. The coastal influence of the Menfi estate near the Tyrrhenian Sea contributes to the wine's distinctive saline mineral character.

milestones.event: First Planeta varietal Grillo from the Menfi coastal estate

wines.description: Planeta's varietal Grillo from Menfi DOC: sea-salt mineral, citrus verbena and herbal from the Tyrrhenian coastal estate.

wines.name: Planeta La Segreta Rosso
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large Slavonian oak casks

taste.summary: La Segreta Rosso is Planeta's multi-varietal entry red: Nero d'Avola blended with Merlot, Syrah and Cabernet Franc for a smooth, approachable and consistent everyday Sicilian red with broad food versatility.

pairings.why: Smooth medium tannin and cherry-herb fruit make this the archetypal pizza red.
A reliable everyday red for classic Italian pasta dishes.
Medium body and herbal notes complement grilled chicken without overpowering.

history.summary: La Segreta Rosso was launched alongside the white in 1997 as Planeta's entry-level multi-varietal red, designed to offer a consistent, approachable style at volume while the prestige cuvees were being established.

milestones.event: La Segreta Rosso launched as Planeta's entry multi-varietal red

wines.description: Planeta's entry-level multi-varietal Sicilian red: Nero d'Avola, Merlot, Syrah and Cabernet Franc in a smooth and broadly approachable everyday blend.

wines.name: Baglio del Cristo di Campobello Adenzia Bianco
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel; cold fermentation

taste.summary: Adenzia Bianco blends Grillo and Inzolia from Baglio del Cristo's chalky Agrigento limestone soils: the combination yields a fresh, almond-citrus white with the mineral depth that characterises this distinctive terroir.

pairings.why: Almond-mineral notes in the wine echo the almond-rich Sicilian couscous tradition.

pairings.dish: Grilled prawns with lemon and herbs
pairings.why: Fresh acidity and citrus blossom complement chargrilled shellfish.
