The wine's almond character bridges the sweet-sour vegetables and toasted almonds in caponata.

history.summary: Baglio del Cristo di Campobello's Adenzia range, named in Sicilian dialect, covers the estate's entry-level whites and reds. The Bianco blends the estate's Grillo and Inzolia from their calcareous chalk soils near Agrigento, 8 kilometres from the sea.

milestones.event: First Adenzia Bianco Grillo-Inzolia from organic Campobello di Licata estate

wines.description: Baglio del Cristo di Campobello's organic Grillo-Inzolia blend: almond-citrus, mineral and fresh from chalky Agrigento limestone soils.

verified.address_quoted: Contrada Favarotta S.S. 123 km 19+200, 92023 Campobello di Licata (AG)

wines.name: Maggio Vini Moscato di Noto
wines.oak_regime: No oak; cold fermentation arrested to preserve sweetness

taste.summary: Maggio Vini's Moscato di Noto is a delicately sweet fortified-adjacent late-harvest Moscato Bianco from the sun-drenched Noto terroir: peach blossom, orange and apricot with balancing acidity from the variety's natural freshness.

pairings.dish: Fresh fruit tart with pastry cream
pairings.why: The wine's peach-apricot sweetness and floral notes are the natural companion for fruit-based pastry.
Moscato di Noto is the traditional Sicilian pairing for almond granita and brioche.
The wine's gentle sweetness and floral character match this Sicilian summer dessert.

history.summary: Maggio Vini produces Moscato di Noto from the Maccari contrada near Syracuse, one of the warmest zones in Sicily. The Noto DOC includes a specific classification for Moscato Bianco, reflecting the ancient Siculo-Arab tradition of sweet wine production in the south-east of the island.

milestones.event: First Maggio Vini Moscato di Noto from the Maccari contrada

wines.description: Maggio Vini's Moscato di Noto: delicately sweet, peach-blossom-apricot, a traditional Sicilian dessert wine from the sun-drenched Maccari-Noto zone.

wines.name: Planeta Burdese
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French barriques (new and second-fill), 16 months

taste.summary: Burdese is Planeta's prestige Cabernet Sauvignon-Franc blend, named in Sicilian dialect for Bordeaux. Grown at Ulmo in Menfi, the wine achieves the concentration of ripeness that Sicily's sun provides while preserving the variety's structural backbone through altitude and careful vinification.

pairings.why: Firm Bordeaux structure and graphite depth are the definitive partners for quality dry-aged beef.
Cedar and tobacco notes with firm tannin complement herb-crusted lamb at its finest.
The wine's mineral depth and concentration pair with truffle's earthy intensity.

history.summary: Planeta produced Burdese from the outset of the winery's modern era, planting Cabernet Sauvignon and Franc at Ulmo with guidance from Jacques Lurton. The name is the Sicilian dialectal word for Bordeaux, emphasising the wine's intentional Bordelais identity.

milestones.event: First Burdese Cabernet blend from Planeta's Ulmo Menfi estate

wines.description: Planeta's prestige Bordeaux-style blend from Menfi, named for Bordeaux in Sicilian dialect: Cabernet Sauvignon and Franc of concentration, graphite depth and structure.

wines.name: Feudi del Pisciotto Grillo
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel only

taste.summary: Feudi del Pisciotto's varietal Grillo from the Val di Noto estate delivers the grape's characteristic citrus-blossom and sea-mineral freshness from a warm inland site moderated by its position on the edge of the Sughereta reserve.

pairings.why: Citrus and sea mineral are the natural accompaniment for simply prepared fresh tuna.
Medium body and high acidity complement a seafood risotto delicately.
A fresh, clean white for grilled squid with lemon and herbs.

history.summary: Feudi del Pisciotto expanded from its red wine focus to add a varietal Grillo, using fruit from their estate near Niscemi to produce an accessible Sicilian white alongside the DOCG and Nero d'Avola red range.

milestones.event: First Feudi del Pisciotto varietal Grillo from the Val di Noto estate

wines.description: Feudi del Pisciotto's varietal Grillo from the Val di Noto: citrus-blossom, sea mineral and fresh from the Niscemi estate.

wines.name: Baglio del Cristo di Campobello Adenzia Rosso
wines.oak_regime: Aged in Slavonian oak, 10 months

taste.summary: Baglio del Cristo's entry-level Adenzia Rosso blends Nero d'Avola's dark fruit warmth with Syrah's peppery spice from the chalky Agrigento limestone estate, producing a food-friendly everyday Sicilian red.

pairings.why: An everyday Sicilian red well suited to sausage and tomato pasta.
Pepper-spice from Syrah and Nero d'Avola warmth complement herb-seasoned grilled lamb.
Full-body and spiced character hold up to a strongly topped pizza.

history.summary: The Adenzia range represents Baglio del Cristo di Campobello's accessible tier below the Lu Patri prestige red, using estate Nero d'Avola blended with Syrah from the chalky Agrigento estate.

milestones.event: First Adenzia Rosso Nero d'Avola-Syrah blend from organic Campobello di Licata estate

wines.description: Baglio del Cristo di Campobello's organic Nero d'Avola-Syrah entry blend: warm, peppery-spiced and an everyday Sicilian red from chalky limestone soils.

verified.address_quoted: Contrada Favarotta S.S. 123 km 19+200, 92023 Campobello di Licata (AG)
